evals.prompt: Run /topics with no arguments to show my full topic registry. The harness is at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/harness.config.json and the research index is at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/research-index.json. When done, write the rendered table and summary line to topics.md in the working directory.
evals.expected_output: A Markdown table written to topics.md showing the one registered topic (Example research topic, harness/example-topic, active, 3 findings, survived: 3) with the correct table header and a summary line '1 topics, 1 with findings.'

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
Table header matches the exact format specified in SKILL.md
The registered topic title appears in the table
The MIF namespace appears in the table
The topic status (active) appears in the table
Verdict rollup shows all 3 findings as survived — proves the namespace join worked
Summary line reports 1 topic with 1 having findings
No error message appears when listing all topics

expectations: The finding count shown in the Findings column is 3, matching the 3 entries in research-index.json for the harness/example-topic namespace.
The table row for 'Example research topic' has all five cells populated — no empty cells in any column.
The summary line appears after the table, not before it.

evals.prompt: Run /topics -t example to resolve the topic matching 'example'. The harness is at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/harness.config.json and the research index is at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/research-index.json. Write the rendered output to topics.md.
evals.expected_output: The output in topics.md shows the matched topic (Example research topic) with its namespace, status, and finding rollup including survived: 3. No 'not found' message appears.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
The matched topic title appears in the output
The MIF namespace of the matched topic appears
Verdict rollup confirms the namespace join ran and all 3 findings survived
No 'not found' error appears when the query matches a topic

expectations: The query 'example' matches the registered topic by title substring ('Example research topic') and that topic's row is shown.
The output is scoped to the single matched topic — it does not render the entire registry with a filter applied on top.
The topic's status (active) is visible somewhere in the output row.

evals.prompt: Run /topics -t zzznotopic to look up a topic that does not exist. The harness is at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/harness.config.json and the research index is at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/research-index.json. Write the full output — including any not-found message and the fallback table — to topics.md.
evals.expected_output: The output in topics.md contains the exact message 'Topic "zzznotopic" not found' and then displays the full topic table as a fallback, per SKILL.md spec.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
Exact not-found message per SKILL.md: Topic "<query>" not found
The full topic table is shown as fallback after the not-found message

expectations: The not-found message appears before the fallback table, not after it.
The fallback table is the complete registry (all topics with rollup data), not an empty table.
The skill does not fabricate or fuzzy-match a topic named 'zzznotopic' — only the exact-literal not-found message and the real registry appear.

evals.prompt: Run /topics --status active to filter the registry to active topics only. The harness is at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/harness.config.json and the research index is at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/research-index.json. Write the rendered table to topics.md.
evals.expected_output: The output in topics.md shows only the active topic (Example research topic, harness/example-topic, active, survived: 3). No non-active topics appear.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
The active topic appears in the filtered output
The namespace of the active topic appears
The status column confirms the topic is active
Verdict rollup for the active topic shows 3 survived findings

expectations: The '--status active' filter returns exactly the one topic that has status=active in harness.config.json.
No topic with a different status (e.g. archived, draft) appears in the output.
The finding rollup for the active topic shows the correct count from the research index.

evals.prompt: Run /topics --status archived to filter the registry to archived topics only. The harness is at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/harness.config.json and the research index is at $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/research-index.json. Write the rendered filtered table to topics.md.
evals.expected_output: The output in topics.md shows no topic rows — the only registered topic is active, not archived. 'Example research topic' and 'harness/example-topic' must not appear.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
The active topic must not appear when filtering by archived status
The active topic's namespace must not appear in the archived-only filter

expectations: The output reflects that no topics have status=archived in harness.config.json, so no data rows appear.
The skill does not fall back to showing all topics when the status filter matches nothing — it shows an empty or zero-row result.
The table header row may still appear but contains no data rows beneath it.
